description: Instant Conversations v2 — Pre-generate and store TTS audio alongside opening text to achieve <500ms time-to-first-audio for all core session types, eliminating TTS from the critical path

userStories.title: Database migration and Storage bucket for v2 audio
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to add audio storage columns to user_progress and create the opening-audio Storage bucket so that the system can store and retrieve pre-generated audio files.
acceptanceCriteria: Create migration file at supabase/migrations/20260222_instant_conversations_v2.sql
Migration adds precomputed_opening_audio_path (TEXT, nullable, default NULL) to user_progress with ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS
Migration adds precomputed_opening_word_timings (JSONB, nullable, default NULL) to user_progress with ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS
Migration includes COMMENT ON COLUMN for both new columns describing their purpose
Migration creates opening-audio Storage bucket (private, not public) using INSERT INTO storage.buckets with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
Existing user_progress rows are not affected (columns default to NULL)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: supabase/migrations/20260222_instant_conversations_v2.sql. Full SQL is in the spec at docs/specs/instant-conversations-spec.md under 'Data Model' section (lines 426-449). Follow patterns from existing migrations in supabase/migrations/. The bucket ID and name are both 'opening-audio'. Use 'public: false' for the bucket. This is the foundation story — non-breaking, just adding nullable columns and an empty bucket.

userStories.title: L1 admin audio generation endpoint
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to generate and store audio files for all L1 illusion openings so that users get near-instant playback when starting L1 core sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/api/admin/generate-opening-audio.post.ts
Endpoint validates X-Admin-Secret header against ADMIN_API_SECRET env var, returns 401 if invalid or missing
When no illusionKey in request body, generates audio for all keys in ILLUSION_OPENING_MESSAGES (Object.keys)
When illusionKey specified in body, generates audio only for that key
When an invalid illusionKey not in ILLUSION_OPENING_MESSAGES is specified, returns 400 with descriptive message
For each key: gets text from ILLUSION_OPENING_MESSAGES[key], calls getTTSProviderFromConfig().synthesize({ text }), uploads audio to Storage at opening-audio/l1/{key}.{ext} with upsert: true
Extension determined by contentType: audio/mpeg → .mp3, else → .wav
After all keys processed, loads existing manifest from Storage opening-audio/l1/manifest.json, merges new results, uploads merged manifest with upsert: true
On TTS failure for a key, logs the error, adds { success: false, error } to results, continues processing other keys — failed key's existing manifest entry is preserved
Response shape: { results: Array<{ illusionKey, success, audioPath?, contentType?, durationMs?, error? }>, manifestUpdated: boolean }
Uses serverSupabaseServiceRole() for Storage operations
All operations logged with [admin-audio] tag: generation start, per-key success/failure, manifest update
Add ADMIN_API_SECRET to runtimeConfig in nuxt.config.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: server/api/admin/generate-opening-audio.post.ts. Detailed server logic is in the spec at docs/specs/instant-conversations-spec.md under 'POST /api/admin/generate-opening-audio' section (lines 580-635). ILLUSION_OPENING_MESSAGES is imported from server/utils/session/ — search for ILLUSION_OPENING_MESSAGES in the codebase to find the exact import path. getTTSProviderFromConfig() is in server/utils/tts/. The manifest format is documented at lines 476-489. The manifest merge strategy: read existing manifest from Storage, spread new results over it, so failed keys keep their existing entries. ADMIN_API_SECRET must be added to runtimeConfig in nuxt.config.ts (check existing env var patterns there).

userStories.title: Unit tests for admin audio generation endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need unit tests for the admin audio generation endpoint to verify auth, TTS integration, manifest management, and error handling.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/unit/server/api/admin/generate-opening-audio.test.ts
Test: valid admin secret with no body → generates audio for all 5 illusion keys, all results success: true, manifest written
Test: valid admin secret with illusionKey: 'stress_relief' → generates audio for 1 key only, manifest merged with existing entries
Test: invalid or missing X-Admin-Secret header → returns 401
Test: TTS failure for one key (e.g., pleasure) → that key shows success: false with error, other 4 keys succeed
Test: manifest contains correct structure per key: audioPath, contentType, timings, timingSource
Test: audio uploaded with upsert: true
Test: manifest preserves existing entries for keys that failed in current run
Test: invalid illusionKey not in ILLUSION_OPENING_MESSAGES → returns 400
Mock getTTSProviderFromConfig() to return a mock provider with synthesize()
Mock serverSupabaseServiceRole() for Storage upload/download operations
Tests pass
userStories.notes: File: tests/unit/server/api/admin/generate-opening-audio.test.ts. Test case table is in the spec at lines 1331-1346. Mock strategy: mock getTTSProviderFromConfig() and Supabase Storage. Follow existing test patterns from tests/unit/ directory. The mock TTS provider.synthesize() should return { audioBuffer: Buffer.from('test'), contentType: 'audio/wav', wordTimings: [{ word: 'Hey', startMs: 0, endMs: 150 }], timingSource: 'estimated', estimatedDurationMs: 5000 }. For manifest download mock, return existing manifest JSON or error for 'not found'. Run with: npm run test:unit.

userStories.title: Extend pre-computation job with audio generation
userStories.description: As the system, I want to generate and store audio alongside the pre-computed opening text for L2/L3 sessions so that the next session can use pre-stored audio playback.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify server/utils/session/precompute-opening.ts to add audio generation after text storage
Text is stored FIRST (existing update call), BEFORE audio generation begins (REQ-20 compliance)
After text storage, call getTTSProviderFromConfig().synthesize({ text: openingText })
Before uploading new audio, read precomputed_opening_audio_path from user_progress and delete previous audio from Storage if it exists
Upload new audio to Storage at opening-audio/l2l3/{userId}/opening.{ext} with upsert: true
Extension determined by contentType: audio/mpeg → .mp3, else → .wav
Update user_progress with precomputed_opening_audio_path (Storage path) and precomputed_opening_word_timings (JSONB: { timings, timingSource, contentType })

If audio generation or upload fails: text is already saved, set precomputed_opening_audio_path and precomputed_opening_word_timings to null (clear stale refs), log failure with [precompute-opening] tag
Stale ref cleanup uses .catch(() => {}) for best-effort (don't throw if cleanup fails)
On success, log with [precompute-opening] tag: userId, illusionKey, nextLayer, audioPath, contentType, durationMs
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: server/utils/session/precompute-opening.ts. Full updated implementation is in the spec at lines 862-946. Key pattern: wrap all audio logic in try/catch AFTER the existing text storage code. The catch block must clear stale audio refs (set both audio fields to null) using .catch(() => {}) for best-effort cleanup. The supabase client is already passed to the function. Use getTTSProviderFromConfig() from server/utils/tts/. Storage operations use supabase.storage.from('opening-audio'). The userId variable is already available in the function scope.

userStories.title: Unit tests for pre-computation audio extension
userStories.description: As a developer, I need unit tests for the audio extension of the pre-computation job to verify text-first ordering, audio lifecycle, and failure isolation.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend existing tests/unit/server/utils/session/precompute-opening.test.ts (or create if not exists)
Test: audio generated and uploaded after text succeeds — audio file at l2l3/{userId}/opening.{ext}, metadata in user_progress
Test: text stored before audio attempt — mock LLM succeeding + TTS failing → precomputed_opening_text is stored, audio fields are null
Test: previous audio deleted before new upload — mock existing precomputed_opening_audio_path → Storage .remove() called before .upload()
Test: audio upload failure → text still saved, audio fields set to null
Test: audio metadata stored correctly in JSONB — { timings, timingSource, contentType }
Test: stale audio refs cleared on audio failure — precomputed_opening_audio_path and word_timings set to null
Mock getTTSProviderFromConfig() and Supabase Storage operations
Tests pass
userStories.notes: File: tests/unit/server/utils/session/precompute-opening.test.ts. Test case table at spec lines 1348-1361. Extend existing mocks for the pre-computation function. Add mock for getTTSProviderFromConfig() returning { synthesize: vi.fn() } and Storage operations (.from().remove(), .from().upload()). Key verification: the ORDER of Supabase calls (text update BEFORE audio operations). Mock the supabase client chain. Run with: npm run test:unit.

userStories.title: Opening endpoint with audio URL and word timings
userStories.description: As the client application, I want the opening endpoint to return pre-stored audio URLs and word timings alongside the text so that I can play pre-stored audio at session start.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/api/session/opening.get.ts (new file replacing opening-text.get.ts)
Delete server/api/session/opening-text.get.ts after creating the new endpoint
Response shape: { text: string|null, source: 'static'|'precomputed'|null, audioUrl: string|null, wordTimings: WordTiming[]|null, contentType: string|null, timingSource: 'actual'|'estimated'|null }

For L1 (intellectual layer): text from ILLUSION_OPENING_MESSAGES, load L1 manifest from Storage with 5-min TTL cache (module-level variable), if manifest entry exists → generate signed URL (300s expiry) and return audioUrl + wordTimings + contentType + timingSource

For L2/L3 (emotional/identity layer): read precomputed_opening_text, precomputed_opening_audio_path, precomputed_opening_word_timings from user_progress. If audio_path exists → generate signed URL (300s expiry), extract timings from JSONB column
If audio is unavailable (no manifest, no audio_path, or createSignedUrl fails): return text with null audio fields (Tier 2 fallback)
Non-core sessions (sessionType !== 'core'): return all nulls
Auth: serverSupabaseUser() for authentication, serverSupabaseServiceRole() for Storage signed URL generation and DB reads
Manifest cache: module-level let variable with 5-min TTL, refreshed on next request after TTL expires
Logging with [instant-start] tag: 'Opening resolved' with { illusionKey, illusionLayer, source, hasText, hasAudio }
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: server/api/session/opening.get.ts (new), deleting server/api/session/opening-text.get.ts. Full implementation details at spec lines 507-578. The manifest caching code is at lines 552-571. Signed URLs via supabase.storage.from('opening-audio').createSignedUrl(path, 300). The new endpoint preserves all v1 query params (illusionKey, illusionLayer, sessionType) and auth pattern. For the select query on user_progress, add precomputed_opening_audio_path and precomputed_opening_word_timings to the select. If createSignedUrl returns an error, gracefully fall back (audioUrl null) — don't throw. READ the existing opening-text.get.ts first to understand the v1 structure, then extend with v2 audio fields.

userStories.title: Unit tests for opening endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need unit tests for the updated opening endpoint to verify L1/L2 paths, manifest caching, signed URLs, and fallback behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/unit/server/api/session/opening.test.ts (migrate from opening-text.test.ts if it exists)
Test: L1 with manifest → returns text, source: 'static', audioUrl (signed URL), wordTimings, contentType, timingSource
Test: L1 without manifest → returns text, source: 'static', all audio fields null
Test: L2 with audio_path in user_progress → returns text, source: 'precomputed', audioUrl, wordTimings, contentType, timingSource
Test: L2 without audio_path → returns text, source: 'precomputed', audio fields null
Test: L2 with no precomputed text → returns all null
Test: non-core session → returns all null
Test: unauthenticated request → 401
Test: invalid illusionKey → 400
Test: manifest cached for 5 minutes (second call within TTL doesn't re-fetch from Storage)
Test: signed URL generated with 300 second expiry
Test: createSignedUrl failure → returns text with null audio fields (graceful Tier 2 fallback)
Mock serverSupabaseUser(), serverSupabaseServiceRole(), Storage download and createSignedUrl
Tests pass
userStories.notes: File: tests/unit/server/api/session/opening.test.ts. Test case table at spec lines 1311-1327. If tests/unit/server/api/session/opening-text.test.ts exists, migrate those tests and extend with v2 tests. Mock strategy at line 1329. Mock Storage download for manifest (return JSON blob or error). Mock createSignedUrl to return { data: { signedUrl: 'https://test.com/signed' }, error: null } or error. For manifest cache testing: call the handler twice within 5 minutes, verify Storage download only called once. Delete old test file if migrating. Run with: npm run test:unit.

userStories.title: playFromUrl() method in useVoiceSession
userStories.description: As the voice session composable, I want a method to play audio from a URL with stored word timings so that pre-stored audio has the same word-by-word highlighting as real-time TTS.
acceptanceCriteria: Add playFromUrl() method to composables/useVoiceSession.ts
Signature: playFromUrl(url: string, text: string, timings: WordTiming[], timingSource: 'actual' | 'estimated'): Promise<boolean>
Creates HTMLAudioElement with the URL directly (no TTS call, no base64)
Sets currentTranscript to the provided text, currentWordIndex to -1, isProcessing to false
On onloadedmetadata: if timingSource is 'estimated', scales word timings to match actual audio duration (same scaling logic as playAIResponse)
On onplay: sets isAISpeaking to true, starts word tracking, resolves Promise with true (resolves on playback START, not end)
On onended: sets isAISpeaking to false, currentWordIndex to last word, stops word tracking (cleanup only, does not gate Promise)
On onerror or play() catch: resolves Promise with false, sets isAISpeaking to false
Cleans up previous audioElement (pause + revokeObjectURL) before creating new one
Export playFromUrl in the composable's return object
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: composables/useVoiceSession.ts. Full implementation code at spec lines 657-733. Key difference from playAIResponse(): (1) no TTS API call — browser fetches audio directly from signed URL, (2) resolves on onplay not onended — needed for 3-second download timeout race in startConversation(). The WordTiming type should already exist (used by playAIResponse). Reuses existing audioElement, wordTimings, playbackStartTime, startWordTracking(), stopWordTracking() from the composable. The timing scaling logic in onloadedmetadata mirrors the existing pattern in playAIResponse.

userStories.title: 3-tier fast-start logic in useVoiceChat
userStories.description: As a user starting a core session, I want the system to use pre-stored audio when available, fall back to real-time TTS, and then to the full flow — so I always get the fastest possible start.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify composables/useVoiceChat.ts startConversation() to implement 3-tier fallback
Update endpoint URL from /api/session/opening-text to /api/session/opening
Tier 1: if opening has audioUrl + wordTimings + contentType, call voiceSession.playFromUrl() wrapped in Promise.race with 3-second timeout
If Tier 1 succeeds (playFromUrl resolves true before timeout): log with [useVoiceChat] tag including downloadMs, return true
If Tier 1 fails (timeout, playFromUrl returns false, or throws): log failure with downloadMs, fall to Tier 2
Tier 2: call voiceSession.playAIResponse(opening.text) — existing v1 fast-start path
If Tier 2 succeeds: log with [useVoiceChat] tag, return true
If Tier 2 fails: log failure, pop the message from messages array, reset isLoading to true, fall through to Tier 3
Tier 3: existing runStreamingWithResilience flow (unchanged code)
Non-core sessions skip fast path entirely (unchanged)
Bootstrap called once (same as v1) regardless of which tier succeeds, runs in background via bootstrapWithRetry
Message pushed to messages array before any audio attempt (same as v1), isLoading set to false before audio attempt
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: File: composables/useVoiceChat.ts. Full updated startConversation() code at spec lines 745-838. The Promise.race for download timeout wraps playFromUrl() and a setTimeout reject at 3000ms. Key state management table at lines 842-850. The existing v1 fast-start code is being restructured into the 3-tier version. Bootstrap is called once before any audio attempt. The opening response from the updated endpoint now includes audioUrl, wordTimings, contentType, timingSource. READ the existing startConversation() code first to understand the current structure, then restructure with the 3-tier logic from the spec.

Simulate events by calling the assigned event handlers (e.g., after calling playFromUrl, trigger audioElement.onplay()). userStories.title: Unit tests for 3-tier fast-start logic
userStories.description: As a developer, I need unit tests for the 3-tier fast-start in useVoiceChat to verify all fallback paths, timeout handling, and state management.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend tests/unit/composables/useVoiceChat-instant-start.test.ts (or create if not exists)
Test: Tier 1 pre-stored audio plays (opening has audioUrl, playFromUrl succeeds → returns true)

Use vi.useFakeTimers() for the 3-second download timeout test

Run with: npm run test:unit.

userStories.title: E2E tests for instant conversations v2
userStories.description: As a developer, I need E2E tests covering the 3-tier fast-start path to verify pre-stored audio playback, TTS fallback, and full flow fallback in the browser.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/e2e/instant-conversations-v2.spec.ts

Test: Tier 1 — mock /api/session/opening returning text + audioUrl + timings, mock audio URL returning audio binary, mock /api/session/bootstrap → audio plays without /api/voice/synthesize being called
Test: Tier 2 — mock /api/session/opening returning text but null audioUrl, mock /api/voice/synthesize returning audio → synthesize called, session works
Test: Tier 1 → Tier 2 fallback — mock opening with audioUrl pointing to 500 error, mock synthesize → fallback works
Test: Tier 3 — mock /api/session/opening returning { text: null }, mock /api/chat with streaming response → full flow used
Test: non-core session skips fast path — navigate to check-in session → /api/session/opening not called
Use page.route() to intercept API calls and audio URL requests
Import test/expect from './fixtures' (NEVER from @playwright/test)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: File: tests/e2e/instant-conversations-v2.spec.ts. Test case table at spec lines 1398-1410. Mock strategy: use page.route() to intercept /api/session/opening, /api/voice/synthesize, /api/session/bootstrap, audio URL, and /api/chat. For the audio binary mock, return a minimal valid WAV or MP3 file (a few bytes with valid header). Import { test, expect } from './fixtures' per CLAUDE.md E2E conventions. Follow existing E2E patterns in tests/e2e/ for auth fixtures and route mocking. Run with: npm run test:e2e.

userStories.title: Per-user audio cleanup on account purge
userStories.description: As the system, I want to delete per-user audio files from Storage when a user's account is purged so that no orphaned data remains.
acceptanceCriteria: Add audio file cleanup to the existing account purge/deletion logic
When a user account is purged, delete all files at opening-audio/l2l3/{userId}/ from Storage using list() then remove() pattern
If no audio files exist for the user, no error occurs (no-op)
If deletion fails, log the failure but do not block account deletion (wrap in try/catch)
L1 static audio is NOT deleted on account purge (not user-specific)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Integrate with existing account purge logic. Search codebase for account deletion/purge handler (may be in server/api/ or server/utils/). Follow the same Storage list+remove pattern used in save-final-recording.post.ts for ceremony preview cleanup (mentioned in spec at line 1291). The Storage path is opening-audio/l2l3/{userId}/ — list files in this prefix, then remove all found file paths. Wrap in try/catch with console.error logging on failure. If no account purge logic exists yet, create a utility function that can be called from the appropriate handler.
